channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I stopped building Postman collections by hand (Claude + Postman MCP)
item.description: If you test backend services, you know the ritual. A new service shows up, you need to poke its endpoints, so you open Postman and start building a collection request by request. Method, URL,...

item.title: RAG Pipeline: Complete Node.js Implementation Guide
item.description: Build production RAG systems in Node.js - Know where it breaks, why it works, and when to use it Introduction: Why Node.js for RAG? 👦 Nephew: Uncle, why would I build RAG in Node.js? I thought...

item.title: The null input that broke my production agent and what fixed it
item.description: The demo ran flawlessly for three weeks. Every test input parsed clean, every output routed correctly, and I thought we had a reliable system. Then a supplier sent a confirmation email with an...

item.title: Independent Research in the Age of Open Science
item.description: For most of modern history, research was strongly tied to institutions. Universities. Research laboratories. Government agencies. Large organizations. These institutions remain extremely important...

item.title: Why New Ideas Often Face Resistance
item.description: Throughout history, many new ideas have encountered resistance. This pattern appears so frequently that it is tempting to treat resistance as evidence that an idea is important. However, the...

item.title: Challenges of Developing a New Conceptual Framework
item.description: Creating a new conceptual framework is often more difficult than it initially appears. The challenge is not simply generating ideas. The challenge is organizing ideas into a structure that remains...

item.title: Cool AI Projects That Failed: The File Integrity Gap
item.description: We ship tools that verify software artifacts. We deal with hashes, checksums, and provenance every day. But looking at the local AI landscape, there is a specific failure mode we see repeatedly:...

item.title: Why I Started Developing the Trinity-Infinity Framework
item.description: Most frameworks begin with a question. The Trinity-Infinity Framework was no exception. It did not start as an attempt to create a grand theory. It started with a simple observation: Many systems...

item.title: Perché niente R&D?
item.description: La domanda colpisce un nervo scoperto dell'economia italiana. Il quadro che emerge dai dati ufficiali è piuttosto netto: l'Italia investe in R&D solo l'1,38% del PIL (2024), contro il 2,24% della...

item.title: Building a Conceptual Framework in Public
item.description: Most conceptual frameworks begin as rough ideas. They are not born as complete systems. They evolve through observation, experimentation, revision, and criticism. For this reason, I believe...

item.title: Siri AI Hands On: A Smart, Helpful Assistant
item.description: The new Siri AI is conversational, omnipresent, and actually helpful.

item.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS...

item.title: SwitchBot’s Standing Circulator Fan is worth fighting for
item.description: I can't remember the last time I got excited about a fan. Normally, I just buy whatever Vornado or Dreo model fits my budget, but that was before I started testing the battery-powered Standing...

item.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
item.description: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

item.title: Nothing cancels this year’s CMF phone due to RAM prices
item.description: Nothing's next budget phone is the latest victim of RAMageddon. As 9to5Google reports, Nothing co-founder Akis Evangelidis announced in a post on X that a follow-up to the CMF Phone 2 Pro won't be...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Victims of Unregulated Squid Fishing
item.description: Dolphins, sharks, turtles, and human workers are all victims of unregulated squid fishing fleets. Another news article. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security...

item.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
item.description: Relativity Space, the rocket company led by former Google executive Eric Schmidt, was picked to launch NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028, as reported earlier by TechCrunch. Under a new...

item.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain
item.description: Security researchers at Paradigm Shift have published a working exploit, dubbed usbliter8, that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips. That code is...

item.title: The Gentlemen RaaS Uses GentleKiller EDR Framework Targeting 400 Security Processes
item.description: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers that it hands out to affiliates for impairing...

item.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
item.description: Smart lighting company Philips Hue has launched its first wired wall modules. Installed behind existing wall switches, the new devices bring non-smart lights into the Hue ecosystem for the first...

item.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
item.description: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a...

item.title: The US banned Anthropic’s Fable 5 release, but the numbers don’t seem to care
item.description: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a...

item.title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
item.description: Microsoft researchers have detailed an exploit chain, named AutoJack, that turns an AI browsing agent into a delivery vehicle for remote code execution. Steer the agent to load an attacker's web...

item.title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
item.description: Reliance is weaving AI into telecom services used by more than 500 million people.

item.title: Operation Endgame Disrupts SocGholish Servers, Cleans 14,971 WordPress Sites
item.description: Dutch law enforcement authorities, along with counterparts from Canada , Germany, and the U.S., have disrupted malicious infrastructure associated with SocGholish and cleaned up nearly 15,000...

item.title: The NTS Radio Player brings the best of internet radio to your hi-fi
item.description: NTS Radio and Swedish audio company Atonemo have teamed up on a dedicated player that brings NTS's genre-defying mixes and streaming stations to almost any stereo or speaker setup. And, like...

item.title: The film about Sam Altman has been dropped by Amazon MGM
item.description: Luca Guadagnino's film about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, Artificial, has reportedly been dropped by Amazon MGM. The film, which stars Andrew Garfield and covers the rollercoaster five days in 2023...

item.title: CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday urged Fortinet customers with FortiGate appliances to take steps to secure against ongoing malicious activity aimed at...

item.title: Our long national sunscreen nightmare is almost over
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: The CEO of Allbirds’ new AI biz has a plan, but no team
item.description: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.
